summary: Converted the Zako Boy and Zako Girl fighter character assembly files from asm/melee/ft/chara into C source/header pairs, updated build lists to compile the new src objects, and added the requested function prototypes. The translated code covers OnDeath, OnLoad, held-item state handling, special-attribute initialization/copying, and calls through existing fighter/item helper functions such as func_80074A4C, func_80070FB4, func_80070C48, func_80070CC4, func_8026B2B4, and func_8026B320.
what_changed: Deleted the old assembly sources asm/melee/ft/chara/ftzakoboy.s and asm/melee/ft/chara/ftzakogirl.s, each removing 172 lines.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftzakoboy.c with C implementations for ftZakoBoy_OnDeath, ftZakoBoy_OnLoad, func_8014F218, func_8014F2F8, func_8014F340, func_8014F388, and func_8014F3FC.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftzakogirl.c with analogous C implementations for ftZakoGirl_OnDeath, ftZakoGirl_OnLoad, func_8014F460, func_8014F540, func_8014F588, func_8014F5D0, and func_8014F624.
Added ftzakoboy.h and ftzakogirl.h including HSD_GObj and Fighter declarations plus prototypes for the newly translated functions.
Changed obj_files.mk so ftzakoboy.o and ftzakogirl.o are built from src/melee/ft/chara instead of asm/melee/ft/chara.
Added the new src object files to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
The OnDeath functions now directly call func_80074A4C(gobj, 0, 0).
The OnLoad functions copy the fighter data ext_attr value into x2D8_specialAttributes2 and set x2D4_specialAttributes to that copied special-attribute storage.
Held-item condition code was expressed in C using Fighter* ft = gobj->user_data, ft->x1974_heldItem, func_8026B2B4 checks, func_8026B320 switch cases, and animation/model helper calls.
smart_moves: Kept Zako Boy and Zako Girl as separate C/header translation units matching their original per-character assembly files, which minimized build-list churn and preserved existing object boundaries.

Used existing Fighter field names such as x1974_heldItem, x2D8_specialAttributes2, x10C_ftData, ext_attr, and x2D4_specialAttributes instead of raw offsets, making the decompiled logic more reusable for future fighter work.
Represented the item-result branch ladder as a switch on func_8026B320 results, clarifying the mapping from cases 1, 2, 3, and 4 to func_80070FB4 arguments 1, 0, 2, and 3.
Added headers with prototypes after review feedback, making the functions available to other translation units and avoiding implicit-declaration style issues.
Updated both obj_files.mk and e_files.mk, which is necessary when replacing assembly objects with C objects in this repository's build setup.
decomp_lessons: For asm-to-C fighter PRs, converting the source file is not enough: the corresponding object path in obj_files.mk must move from asm/... to src/..., and epilogue object tracking may need to be updated.
When a reviewer asks for prototypes, add them to the character-specific header rather than leaving functions only defined in the .c file.
Special-attribute setup in fighter OnLoad code can involve copying from ft->x10C_ftData->ext_attr into ft->x2D8_specialAttributes2, then assigning ft->x2D4_specialAttributes to that copied buffer.
Held-item dependent fighter logic can often be made readable by naming the gobj user_data as Fighter* ft and using the known held-item field instead of preserving raw 0x1974 accesses.
Near-duplicate character files can be translated in parallel, but keep symbol names character-specific where known, such as ftZakoBoy_OnDeath versus ftZakoGirl_OnDeath.
The local variable named unused appears in both main switch functions but is not used in the shown C; this may have been matching-driven, but the PR context does not explain the rationale.
naming_conventions: Known exported lifecycle names use character casing: ftZakoBoy_OnDeath, ftZakoBoy_OnLoad, ftZakoGirl_OnDeath, ftZakoGirl_OnLoad.
Unknown functions remain address-based func_8014F218, func_8014F2F8, func_8014F340, func_8014F388, func_8014F3FC, func_8014F460, func_8014F540, func_8014F588, func_8014F5D0, and func_8014F624.
Character file names are lowercase ftzakoboy.c/.h and ftzakogirl.c/.h, while public known symbols use ftZakoBoy/ftZakoGirl capitalization.
Header guards follow the existing lowercase underscore style shown in the diff: _ftzakoboy_h_ and _ftzakogirl_h_.
Local names in the translation include gobj for HSD_GObj*, ft for Fighter*, sa2 for specialAttributes2 storage, result for func_8026B2B4, and switched_res for func_8026B320.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The assembly cmpwi/bge/beq branch tree over func_8026B320 return values was translated as a C switch with explicit cases 1 through 4 and no default action.
The C preserves the conditional call structure: only run the switch and optional func_80070C48 call when func_8026B2B4(ft->x1974_heldItem) returns 0.
Simple wrapper functions were reduced to direct calls where possible, such as OnDeath calling func_80074A4C(gobj, 0, 0).
The special-attribute copyback helpers cast x2D4_specialAttributes to u32* before assigning *ft->x10C_ftData->ext_attr, matching a raw word copy pattern from the assembly.
Both boy and girl translations preserve analogous helper-call behavior, differing mainly by symbol addresses/names and original source ordering.
Evidence for exact compiler-matching strategy is limited to the diff; no reviewer comments discuss why specific locals or casts were required.

review_feedback.comment: Please add the function prototypes to the respective headers, then this PR should be good..
review_feedback.resolution_evidenced: The PR added ftzakoboy.h and ftzakogirl.h with prototypes for all functions introduced in the corresponding C files.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_8014F218 or the analogous Zako Girl functions to semantic names.
Search for other fighter OnLoad implementations that copy x10C_ftData->ext_attr into x2D8_specialAttributes2.
Look for subsequent cleanup of the unused local in func_8014F218 or func_8014F460.
Compare ftzakoboy/ftzakogirl item handling with other fighter held-item callbacks using func_8026B2B4 and func_8026B320.
Check whether later header guard conventions changed away from _ftzakoboy_h_ style.
